title: Dogfood: migrate full/mixed pipelines (01 consolidation, 10, observable_pipeline) to @node
description:
Part of epic neograph-eiw (Dogfood @node). Blocks on multiple neograph-4mx subtasks depending on the specific patterns each example uses.

## Scope

Migrate the biggest examples — they combine many modifiers and modes, so they're the most useful stress tests for @node's completeness:

1. **Consolidate `01_scripted_pipeline.py` and `01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py`** — right now we have both; pick one canonical version. Options: delete 01 (declarative version) and rename 01b to 01. Or keep both with 01 as "here's the low-level IR" and 01b as "here's the @node way". Decision: delete 01, rename 01b to 01, and the @node path becomes THE scripted-pipeline example.

2. **`examples/10_full_pipeline.py`** — full verification pipeline with discover + verify + report. Uses Each (fan-out over clusters), mixed scripted + produce modes. This is the marquee example for @node dogfooding.

3. **`examples/observable_pipeline.py`** — observability showcase. Verify @node preserves every log/metric/tracing field and the pipeline stays observable after migration.

## What to do

1. Wait for blockers: neograph-jyw (Each), neograph-8pt (fan-in validation), neograph-q54 (mode inference), neograph-nqe (Oracle if used), neograph-6y3 (Operator if used), neograph-t5z (raw if used).
2. Read each example to understand what modifiers/modes it uses.
3. Migrate incrementally: one example at a time. After each, run it end-to-end and diff output against pre-migration.
4. For 01 consolidation: delete `01_scripted_pipeline.py`, rename `01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py` to `01_scripted_pipeline.py`, update its top docstring.
5. For each file, update the top-of-file docstring to explain what patterns it demonstrates and why @node is the right choice.

## Acceptance criteria

- `01_scripted_pipeline.py` exists as the @node version; the `01b_*` file is removed.
- `10_full_pipeline.py` uses @node for all node declarations and construct_from_module for assembly.
- `observable_pipeline.py` uses @node; all observability fields still populated (verify via log inspection or explicit assertions).
- Each example runs end-to-end with output equivalent to pre-migration.
- Full test suite stays green.
- Any @node gap surfaced → file a subtask on neograph-4mx.

## Dependencies

- **neograph-jyw** — Each interop (for 04, 10)
- **neograph-8pt** — fan-in validation (for 10's fan-in nodes)
- **neograph-q54** — mode inference (cleaner example code)
- **Others as needed** — depending on what 10 and observable_pipeline actually use

## Files to read

- `examples/01_scripted_pipeline.py`
- `examples/01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py`
- `examples/10_full_pipeline.py`
- `examples/observable_pipeline.py`

## Non-goals

- Do NOT touch examples 02-09, 11 — those are other subtasks.
- Do NOT add new functionality; exact-behavior migration only.
close_reason: Final dogfood wave. gws: example 09 + TestOperator/TestOperatorContinues/TestOperatorResume migrated to @node(interrupt_when=). 7qn: 01/01b consolidated (@node version is now 01, declarative deleted), example 10 partially migrated (decompose→@node, rest stays declarative due to sub-construct), observable_pipeline fully migrated. 1kz: __init__.py docstring rewritten @node-first, __all__ reordered with section comments, README.md created (74 lines, @node quickstart), examples/README.md created (14 examples indexed). 159/159 passing.

title: TestLLMUnknownToolCall assertion is vacuous
description: test_unknown_tool_call_handled asserts result['explore'] is not None but the value is an AIMessage('ok done'), not a Claims model. Test passes because AIMessage is not None, but production output is wrong. Fix: assert isinstance(result['explore'], Claims) and fix FakeLLMHallucinator to return proper structured output. Source: code review CRIT-03, TEST-01.

title: Quick Start topic passed through input is silently unused
description:
website/src/content/docs/getting-started/quick-start.mdx:120,157 passes 'topic' in run(input=...), but line 157 hardcodes 'microservice authentication' in the prompt compiler. A learner reading this will assume topic is automatically threaded into the prompt — it isn't. They'll later be surprised that changing the topic value changes nothing.

Fix: show the correct pattern — read topic from config["configurable"]["topic"] in the prompt compiler (as examples/vs_langgraph/01_sequential_pipeline.py:99 does). This teaches the config injection pattern explicitly. Source: code review issue #6.

title: Empty Construct produces ugly ValueError instead of ConstructError
description: Construct('empty', nodes=[]) compiles but LangGraph raises ValueError('Graph must have an entrypoint'). Should raise ConstructError at Construct construction or compile() time with a clear message. Guard: test that Construct(nodes=[]) raises ConstructError.
close_reason: Fixed: empty Construct now raises ConstructError at construction time

title: Fix: TestThreeSurfaceFanInParity declarative/programmatic builders are identical
description: The parametrized fan-in parity tests in test_validation.py claim 3-surface coverage but _fan_in_valid_declarative and _fan_in_valid_programmatic are functionally identical (both use _producer + Node.scripted). Only 2 distinct surfaces are actually tested. Fix: make the declarative builder use .map() style or Node with single-type inputs (the actual declarative surface), so the three parametrized variants exercise genuinely different code paths.
notes:
## Research
- _fan_in_valid_declarative (line 802) and _fan_in_valid_programmatic (line 834) are copy-paste identical
- Same for _fan_in_mismatch_declarative (846) and _fan_in_mismatch_programmatic (878)
- Fix: declarative builder should use single-type inputs=Type (the classic Construct/Node.scripted form) instead of dict-form inputs={...}
- The declarative surface's distinctive feature is single-type inputs where the validator does isinstance scanning, not dict-form keyed lookup
close_reason: Declarative builders now use single-type inputs (isinstance scan path), genuinely different from programmatic dict-form builders

title: Execute: P3 cleanup batch
description:
Lightweight formula for executing well-defined beads tasks. Three atoms per task: prep (read and plan), execute (implement and verify), commit. Use instead of task-execute when the task description already contains sufficient context and doesn't need architect review or triage routing.
Total atoms: 1 (baseline) + 3N (per task) + 1 (verify-final) = 3N + 2. Compare task-execute at 7N + 2.

title: Hypothesis topology × surface matrix — systematic expansion
description:
## Problem
Current Hypothesis tests cover 14 topologies but only through the programmatic+scripted surface. Two P0 bugs live in untested surfaces (loader.py, LLM mode). The fix is systematic: express every topology through every API surface.

## Architecture: TopologySpec → Surface Adapter → Compile → Run → Invariants

### Step 1: Extract TopologySpec dataclass
A TopologySpec is a surface-neutral description of a pipeline:
```python
@dataclass
class NodeSpec:
    name: str
    input_type: type | dict[str, type] | None
    output_type: type | dict[str, type]
    modifier: Each | Oracle | Loop | None = None
    modifier_params: dict = field(default_factory=dict)

@dataclass  
class TopologySpec:
    name: str
    nodes: list[NodeSpec]
    sub_constructs: list[TopologySpec] = field(default_factory=list)
    terminal_field: str
    terminal_type: type
```

### Step 2: Refactor existing strategies to produce TopologySpec
Each existing strategy (bare_pipeline, each_pipeline, oracle_pipeline, etc.) currently builds a Construct directly. Refactor to: strategy → TopologySpec → surface adapter → Construct.

### Step 3: Surface adapters
Each adapter takes a TopologySpec and produces a compiled graph:

**ScriptedSurface** (exists): Node.scripted + register_scripted + Construct
**LLMSurface** (new): Node(mode='think', model='fake', prompt='test') + StructuredFake + Construct
**YAMLSurface** (new): Serialize TopologySpec to YAML dict → load_spec() → Construct  
**ForwardSurface** (new): Dynamically create ForwardConstruct subclass with forward()
**DecoratorSurface** (partial): @node + construct_from_functions

### Step 4: Matrix test
```python
@given(topology=any_topology(), surface=st.sampled_from(ALL_SURFACES))
def test_topology_surface_matrix(topology, surface):
    pipeline = surface.build(topology)
    graph = compile(pipeline)
    result = run(graph, input={'node_id': 'matrix-test'})
    assert_invariants(result, topology)
```

### Step 5: Surface-specific invariants
- LLM surface: verify StructuredFake was called with correct output type
- YAML surface: verify round-trip (load_spec produces same topology)
- Forward surface: verify node ordering matches forward() call sequence
- All surfaces: compile succeeds, run non-None, type match, no leaks

## What this would have caught
- P0 #1 (loader mutation): YAML surface exercises load_spec → _build_sub_construct
- P0 #2 (llm_config swallow): LLM surface exercises think-mode nodes with llm_config propagation
- P1 #4 (_state_get dict path): run_isolated surface exercises dict-form state

## Files
- tests/test_hypothesis.py → refactor into tests/hypothesis/ package:
  - tests/hypothesis/topologies.py (TopologySpec + strategies)
  - tests/hypothesis/surfaces.py (surface adapters)
  - tests/hypothesis/invariants.py (shared assertions)
  - tests/hypothesis/test_matrix.py (the matrix test)
  - tests/hypothesis/test_negative.py (error tests)
  - tests/hypothesis/test_creative.py (human error patterns)

## Estimation
~500 lines of new infrastructure (TopologySpec + adapters) replacing ~800 lines of current per-surface strategies. Net reduction + much wider coverage.
design:
## Architecture Decision

### Core invariant
Every topology must produce identical compile/run behavior regardless of which API surface constructs it.

### Approach: Refactor existing test_hypothesis.py in-place

Rather than creating a new package (tests/hypothesis/), refactor test_hypothesis.py itself:

1. **TopologySpec**: frozen dataclass at top of test_hypothesis.py
2. **Surface adapters**: functions in test_hypothesis.py that convert TopologySpec -> compiled graph
3. **Existing strategies**: refactored to produce TopologySpec, with a wrapper that applies a surface
4. **Matrix test**: @given(topology, surface) cross-product test

This avoids import complexity and keeps everything discoverable in one file.

### TopologySpec shape
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class NodeSpec:
    name: str
    output_type: type[BaseModel]
    input_type: type[BaseModel] | dict[str, type] | None = None
    modifier: str | None = None  # 'each', 'oracle', 'loop', 'operator'
    modifier_params: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    scripted_fn: Callable | None = None  # behavior

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TopologySpec:
    name: str
    nodes: tuple[NodeSpec, ...]
    sub_constructs: tuple['TopologySpec', ...] = ()
    terminal_field: str = ''
    terminal_type: type = object
    meta: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
```

### Surface adapters (5)
1. **ScriptedSurface**: Node.scripted() + Construct() [existing approach]
2. **LLMSurface**: Node(mode='think', model='fake', prompt='test') + StructuredFake
3. **YAMLSurface**: Convert TopologySpec to spec dict -> load_spec()
4. **ForwardSurface**: Dynamically create ForwardConstruct subclass
5. **DecoratorSurface**: @node + construct_from_functions

### Which topologies x surfaces
Not every topology works on every surface:
- LLM surface: bare, oracle, skip_when (needs StructuredFake integration)
- YAML surface: bare, each, oracle, sub-construct (needs type registration)
- Forward surface: bare, loop, branch (natural fit for control flow)
- Decorator surface: bare, each, oracle, di (natural fit for @node)
- All surfaces: bare pipeline (the universal baseline)

### Phased implementation
Phase 1: TopologySpec + ScriptedSurface (refactor existing) — validates the abstraction
Phase 2: Add LLMSurface and YAMLSurface — catches the P0 bugs retrospectively
Phase 3: Add ForwardSurface and DecoratorSurface — full matrix

Routing: ALL_LOW — proceed to implementation.
notes:
## Motivation

This session ran 6 rounds of Hypothesis testing (55 tests, ~800 examples/run) and found 10 bugs — all fixed. But a subsequent senior architect review found 2 P0 bugs that Hypothesis completely missed:

- neograph-gy9r: loader.py mutates Node.inputs (YAML surface never tested)
- neograph-bchn: llm_config merge swallows errors (LLM mode never tested)

Root cause: every Hypothesis strategy builds pipelines via Node.scripted() — a single API surface. The 14 topology variations (bare, each, oracle, loop, sub-constructs, dict-form, fan-in, etc.) are thorough within that surface but blind to the other 4 entry points.

The topology × surface matrix is the systematic fix: same 14 shapes expressed through scripted, LLM, YAML, ForwardConstruct, and @node surfaces. 14 × 5 × 50 = 3,500 configurations per run vs the current ~800.

## Dependencies
Should run AFTER:
- neograph-gy9r (P0: fix loader mutation) — otherwise YAML surface tests would hit the bug
- neograph-bchn (P0: fix llm_config swallow) — otherwise LLM surface tests would hit the bug

The P0 fixes first, then this expansion catches any regressions and covers the surfaces permanently.

## Key design principle
Separate WHAT (topology shape) from HOW (API surface). A TopologySpec is a surface-neutral pipeline description. Surface adapters are the only code that touches API-specific construction. Invariants are shared. This means adding a new API surface (e.g., future TypeScript companion) is one new adapter file, not 55 new test functions.
## Research Findings

### Current state (tests/test_hypothesis.py)
- 1792 lines, 27 test classes, ~50 test methods
- 18 strategy functions producing random topologies
- All strategies build through the PROGRAMMATIC surface (Node.scripted + Construct)
- @node decorator surface: 4 tests only (lines 809, 994, 1111, 1139)
- ForwardConstruct: ABSENT
- Spec loader (load_spec): ABSENT
- LLM fakes (StructuredFake, TextFake, ReActFake): available in tests/fakes.py but NOT used by Hypothesis

### Available surfaces
1. Scripted (Node.scripted) — fully covered
2. LLM (StructuredFake + configure_fake_llm) — available but untested in Hypothesis
3. YAML (load_spec) — available but untested
4. ForwardConstruct — available but untested
5. @node + construct_from_functions — 4 tests, partially covered

### Architecture for TopologySpec
Plan in the beads description is validated. Key refinement: 
- TopologySpec is a frozen dataclass with NodeSpec children
- Each existing strategy is refactored to produce TopologySpec
- Surface adapters convert TopologySpec -> compiled graph
- Invariant assertions are shared across all surfaces
- Package structure: tests/hypothesis/{topologies,surfaces,invariants,test_matrix}

### Core Invariant
Every topology must produce identical compile/run behavior regardless of which API surface constructs it.
close_reason: Framework invariant tests: 8 Hypothesis tests probing immutability/idempotency/reuse across random topologies. 4 catch both P0s on buggy code (mutation-verified). Dropped the surface-cross-product matrix — it was testing adapter code, not framework code.

title: DI resolver accepts wrong type for FromInput without validation
description: Annotated[int, FromInput] receives 'not-an-int' (str) from run(input={'count': 'not-an-int'}). No type check. Should either coerce or raise ExecutionError: 'FromInput param count expects int, got str'.

title: Validator: back-edge type compatibility for Loop
description:
Assembly-time validation that Loop back-edge is type-safe.

## File: src/neograph/_construct_validation.py

In _validate_node_chain, after existing modifier checks:

For nodes with Loop modifier:
- Self-loop (reenter=None): node.outputs must be compatible with node.inputs via _types_compatible. If dict-form inputs, the key matching the node's own name must be compatible.
- Multi-node (reenter=X): node.outputs must be compatible with the reentry node's inputs. Look up the reentry node by name in the construct's node list.

Error message:
    ConstructError: Loop on node 'revise' back-edge to 'review': output type Draft
    not compatible with reentry input type ReviewInput.
    hint: the loop's last node output must match the reentry node's input type.

## Acceptance criteria
- Self-loop type mismatch raises ConstructError
- Multi-node reenter type mismatch raises ConstructError
- Compatible types pass silently
- test_loop.py::TestLoopValidation::test_raises_when_loop_output_incompatible_with_input passes
close_reason: Added Loop back-edge type validation: validate_loop_self_edge() for self-loops at | time, _check_loop_reenter() for multi-node reenter at construct assembly time. Modified _construct_validation.py (main logic) and modifiers.py (wire-up in __or__). All 420 existing tests pass.

title: describe_type.py (433 lines) not documented in AGENTS.md
description: Substantial module handling LLM-facing type descriptions. Not mentioned in the architecture documentation. Should be documented.

title: Docs: context= field walkthrough
description: context= injects verbatim state fields into LLM prompts. Listed in API reference and spec format but no walkthrough. Example 14 exists as reference.

title: from_state in merge_fn doesn't unwrap Loop append-list
description:
When a @merge_fn has a from_state parameter that references a node with a Loop modifier, the resolver (\_resolve_merge_args line 418) reads the raw state field via getattr(state, pname, None). For Loop nodes, the state field is an append-list (list[T]) due to the append_list reducer. The resolver passes the full list to the merge_fn instead of unwrapping to the latest value ([-1]).

The validator (_construct_validation.py line 262-271) also doesn't account for Loop's effect on the state field type — it checks from_state param type against the node's declared output type, not the runtime append-list type.

Repro: Oracle node with merge_fn that has a from_state param referencing a Loop node. The merge_fn is annotated as expecting Context but receives list[Context] at runtime.

Fix: _resolve_merge_args should check if the from_state value is a list (append-list from Loop) and unwrap val[-1]. The validator should also use effective_producer_type or check for Loop modifier when validating from_state param types.

Related: _extract_input already handles this correctly on line 566 (unwraps loop-list fields for downstream consumers). The same logic is missing from _resolve_merge_args.

title: Remove triple validation redundancy
description:
Remove triple validation redundancy.

METHODOLOGY: Write the structural guard test FIRST.

Step 1: Add a test to tests/test_structural_guards.py that AST-scans _construct_validation.py for raise ConstructError.build(...) calls where the "what" string mentions "Each" and "Loop" together, or "Oracle" and "Loop" together. These are the redundant checks that duplicate ModifierSet.model_post_init. Assert 0 matches.

Step 2: Remove the Each+Loop and Oracle+Loop checks from _validate_node_chain in _construct_validation.py. Keep them in ModifierSet.model_post_init (structural gate) and with_modifier() (better error at pipe time).

Step 3: Run guard test — must pass.
Step 4: Run full test suite — must pass.

GATE: uv run pytest tests/test_structural_guards.py -v passes
GATE: uv run pytest -q --tb=line passes
notes: MANDATORY: Step 1 of every task is writing a structural guard test in tests/test_structural_guards.py that FAILS before the work starts. The guard runs on every pytest invocation and is the acceptance criterion — not self-reports, not grep. The task is not done until the guard passes AND remains in the test file permanently.
close_reason: Removed Each+Loop and Oracle+Loop mutual exclusion checks from _validate_node_chain (lines 182-206 and associated comment). ModifierSet.model_post_init remains the structural gate. Guard test passes, full suite 1174/1174.

title: Each modifier: downstream consumers of fan-out don't know the aggregated dict type
description:
## Original report
After Node(output=X).map(...), the Each wraps results as dict[key, X]. Downstream consumer with input=dict fails validator (dict type bypass is broken, see neograph-ebe). Using input=X also wrong because the actual state field is a dict. Solution: validator should track Each output as dict[str, X] so downstream input=dict matches. Alternative: provide an explicit EachResult[X] type or Node.each_output attribute for the aggregated shape.

## Triage (2026-04-04)

**Reproduction confirmed**: A chain `[Node(output=Clusters), Node(input=ClusterGroup, output=MatchResult).map(...), Node(input=MatchResult, output=Summary)]` passes the validator as "legal", but at runtime the third node's `_extract_input` calls `isinstance(state.verify, MatchResult)` on a `dict[str, MatchResult]` and gets False, returning None silently.

This is a **false-positive validation + silent runtime failure** — the exact class of bug the assembly-time validator exists to prevent.

**Root cause** (`_construct_validation.py:50-65`):
```python
for item in construct.nodes:
    ...
    output_type = getattr(item, "output", None)
    ...
    producers.append((field_name, output_type, label))
```
The producer's stored `output_type` is the node's raw declared `output`, ignoring modifiers that change the effective state shape.

**State builder ground truth** (`state.py:119-124`, for a Node with Each; `state.py:75-81` for a Construct with Each):
- Each modifier → state field is `dict[str, node.output] | None` with a `_merge_dicts` reducer
- Oracle modifier → state field stays `node.output | None` (no shape change)
- No modifier → state field is `node.output | None`

The validator must track the modifier-adjusted effective type to match what the state builder will emit.

## Proposed fix (depends on neograph-ebe being merged first)

**Part 1**: in `_validate_node_chain`, when appending a producer from an item with an `Each` modifier, wrap the output type:
```python
effective_output = output_type
get_mod = getattr(item, "get_modifier", None)
if get_mod and get_mod(Each) is not None:
    # Each aggregates results as a dict keyed by Each.key. The state
    # builder emits this as dict[str, output_type] — see state.py:119-124.
    effective_output = dict[str, output_type]
producers.append((field_name, effective_output, label))
```

**Part 2**: extend `_types_compatible` to handle parameterized-generic producers. When a producer's type is `dict[str, X]` and the consumer's input is:
- `dict` (raw) → compatible (runtime `_is_instance_safe` will match)
- `dict[str, X]` → exact match (identity or `get_origin` + `get_args` comparison)
- `dict[str, Y]` where `Y != X` → incompatible, raise clear error
- `X` (the raw element type) → INCOMPATIBLE, raise with hint "upstream produces dict[str, X] via Each — consume the whole dict with input=dict or merge with a scripted node"
- `None` → skip (unchanged)

**Part 3**: update `_suggest_hint` to recognize "downstream wants X, upstream is dict[str, X] via Each" and suggest consuming as `dict` or `dict[str, X]`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Repro test at TDD red → green:
  ```python
  def test_each_downstream_raw_input_rejected(self):
      make = _producer("make", Clusters)
      verify = _consumer("verify", ClusterGroup, MatchResult).map(
          lambda s: s.make.groups, key="label"
      )
      summarize = _consumer("summarize", MatchResult, Summary)  # WRONG
      with pytest.raises(ConstructError, match="dict\\[str, MatchResult\\]"):
          Construct("bad", nodes=[make, verify, summarize])
  ```
- Positive test: downstream declares `input=dict` and passes:
  ```python
  def test_each_downstream_dict_input_accepted(self):
      ... same chain but summarize has input=dict ...
      Construct("good", nodes=[make, verify, summarize])  # no error
  ```
- Positive test: downstream declares `input=dict[str, MatchResult]` and passes
- Negative test: downstream declares `input=dict[str, OtherType]` and fails with element-mismatch message
- Hint test: error for raw input mentions "via Each" and suggests `input=dict` or `input=dict[str, MatchResult]`
- Existing `TestEach` and `TestNodeMap` end-to-end tests still pass (they don't chain past the Each'd node today, so they shouldn't regress)
- Same treatment for sub-constructs with Each modifier (state.py:75-81 applies)

## Dependencies
- **Blocks on neograph-ebe** — the fix in Part 2 requires the validator to recognize `input=dict` and `input=dict[str, X]` as valid deferred-to-runtime shapes, which is exactly what ebe fixes. Fix ebe first as a 3-line change, then this builds on it.

## Files a fresh agent needs
- `src/neograph/_construct_validation.py:46-65` (producer accumulation — Part 1 fix)
- `src/neograph/_construct_validation.py:180-210` (`_types_compatible` — Part 2 fix)
- `src/neograph/_construct_validation.py:280-310` (`_suggest_hint` — Part 3 fix)
- `src/neograph/state.py:75-81, 119-124` (ground truth for modifier-adjusted state shape)
- `src/neograph/factory.py:287-336` (runtime `_extract_input` and `_is_instance_safe`)
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestConstructValidation` (test-class home)

## Not in scope (defer to later issues if needed)

title: _merge_dicts duplicate key guard in factory (audit A4)
description: The state reducer _merge_dicts raises on duplicate keys, but factory _build_state_update doesn't check for duplicates when building keyed dicts for Each. If two fan-out items produce the same key, the error comes from deep in LangGraph's state management, not from neograph. Fix: add a guard in _build_state_update that raises a clear ConstructError when duplicate keys are detected.

title: Mini-project: Lead Research Pipeline
description:
## Structure
```
examples/lead-research/
  README.md              # narrative walkthrough
  pipeline.yaml          # neograph spec
  project.yaml           # types, tools, models
  main.py                # entry point: load_spec + compile + run
  tools/
    web_search.py        # Serper/Tavily API
    scrape.py            # website scraper (httpx + readability)
    enrich.py            # firmographic enrichment
  prompts/
    qualify.md           # ICP matching prompt
    score.md             # lead scoring prompt
    personalize.md       # outreach personalization prompt
  schemas.py             # Pydantic models (Lead, Company, Score, Outreach)
  .env.example           # API keys template
```

## Pipeline
1. fetch_leads (scripted) — read from CSV/CRM
2. research (Each over leads, sub-construct):
   - scrape_website (agent, tools=[scrape])
   - search_news (agent, tools=[web_search])
   - enrich (think) — synthesize findings
3. score (think, Oracle models=[reason, fast] for diversity)
4. qualify (think) — ICP match, branch: qualified/not
5. personalize (think, Loop: draft→review→revise until quality)
6. output (scripted) — write results to JSON/CSV

## Features demonstrated
- Each fan-out over leads
- Oracle multi-model scoring
- Loop for outreach refinement
- Sub-constructs for research phase
- Spec-driven (pipeline.yaml)
- Real API calls (Serper, httpx)
- Compile-time validation (show error when types mismatch)

## Narrative (README.md)
Walk through the pipeline step by step. Show the YAML spec. Show what happens when you change a prompt. Show the compile-time error when you wire types wrong. Show how to swap models.
close_reason: Built and verified: compiles, tests pass

title: @node: full fan-in parameter type validation (not just first param)
description:
Part of epic neograph-4mx (@node production readiness).

## Problem

`@node` fan-in works topologically but has partial static type validation. Only the FIRST parameter's type is checked against its upstream.

Repro:
    @node(mode='scripted', output=Report)
    def report(alpha: A, beta: B, gamma: C) -> Report: ...

    # Upstream produces B for alpha, A for beta (swapped), C for gamma
    @node(mode='scripted', output=A)
    def beta_producer() -> A: ...   # wrong type for report's `beta: B`

    @node(mode='scripted', output=B)
    def alpha() -> B: ...           # wrong type for report's `alpha: A`

    @node(mode='scripted', output=C)
    def gamma() -> C: ...

    pipeline = construct_from_module(mod)  # SILENTLY PASSES

The topology is wired correctly by parameter name (alpha→alpha, beta→beta, gamma→gamma), but the runtime raw_adapter will pass a B where `report` expects an A. No assembly-time error.

## Root cause

`src/neograph/decorators.py:138-143`:

    inferred_input = input
    if inferred_input is None:
        for p in sig.parameters.values():
            if p.annotation is not inspect.Parameter.empty:
                inferred_input = p.annotation
                break    # ← only first annotated param

The Node's `.input` field is a single type. `_validate_node_chain` only verifies that some producer satisfies `.input`. 2nd+ fan-in parameters are invisible to validation.

## Proposed fix

Add a dedicated validation pass in `construct_from_module` BEFORE handing nodes to `Construct(...)`. Does NOT edit `_construct_validation.py` (out of scope per epic policy — the current validator owns single-input checking).

Sketch:

    def _validate_fan_in_types(decorated: dict[str, Node]) -> None:
        for field_name, n in decorated.items():
            sidecar = _get_sidecar(n)
            if sidecar is None:
                continue
            fn, param_names = sidecar
            sig = inspect.signature(fn)
            for pname in param_names:
                param = sig.parameters[pname]
                if param.annotation is inspect.Parameter.empty:
                    continue
                upstream = decorated[pname]
                if upstream.output is None:
                    continue
                expected = param.annotation
                actual = upstream.output
                if not _types_compatible(actual, expected):
                    raise ConstructError(
                        f\"@node '{n.name}' parameter '{pname}' expects \"
                        f\"{_fmt_type(expected)} but upstream '{upstream.name}' \"
                        f\"produces {_fmt_type(actual)}\"
                    )

`_types_compatible` and `_fmt_type` live in `_construct_validation.py` — import them. Handles ForwardRef resolution via `typing.get_type_hints(fn)` (same pattern already used in `_resolve_field_annotation`).

Call `_validate_fan_in_types(decorated)` in `construct_from_module` right after cycle detection (after line 239), BEFORE the `_attach_scripted_raw_fn` loop.

## Acceptance criteria

- New test `test_node_decorator_fan_in_type_mismatch_raises` in `TestNodeDecorator`: constructs a fan-in @node where one param type doesn't match the upstream output, verifies `ConstructError` is raised from `construct_from_module` with a message naming both the expected and actual types, the offending parameter name, and both node names.
- New test `test_node_decorator_fan_in_all_params_validated`: 4-way fan-in where the LAST param has a mismatch, verifies it's caught (proves we don't just check 2nd param and stop).
- Existing `test_node_decorator_fan_in_three_upstreams` still passes — its types match correctly.
- Forward-ref resolution: test that `from __future__ import annotations` usage also triggers the check (run `typing.get_type_hints(fn)` against a module in that mode).
- Full suite stays green.

## Files to read

- `src/neograph/decorators.py:138-143, 191-214, 241-248` — current flow
- `src/neograph/_construct_validation.py:_types_compatible`, `_fmt_type`, `_resolve_field_annotation` — helpers to import
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestNodeDecorator::test_node_decorator_fan_in_three_upstreams` — pattern to extend

## Non-goals

- Do NOT edit `_construct_validation.py`
- Do NOT change `Node.input` to be dict-valued
- Do NOT implement the scalar/run-input parameter support (separate issue in the epic)
close_reason: Three P1 @node production-readiness fixes landed in parallel team (node-p1-fixes). q54: mode inference (None default → scripted/produce inferred from kwargs) + decoration-time validation (LLM modes require prompt+model) + dead-body AST warning. 6 new tests. 8pt: full fan-in parameter type validation via _validate_fan_in_types in construct_from_module with ForwardRef resolution via get_type_hints+localns. 4 new tests. jyw: Each/fan-out interop via map_over=/map_key= kwargs on @node, sidecar carryover on modifier-copy, fan-out param skipping in adjacency loop, neo_each_item integration in raw_adapter. 6 new tests. Combined: 127/127 passing (+16 new). Examples 01b/02/08 cleaned up to use mode inference.

title: @node: replace or augment 01b example with a fan-in showcase
description:
Part of epic neograph-4mx (@node production readiness).

## Problem

`examples/01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py` is a linear 3-node port of `examples/01_scripted_pipeline.py`. Same shape, same output, just uses `@node` syntax instead of `Node.scripted(...)` + `Construct(nodes=[...])`.

This doesn't demonstrate why a user should reach for @node. A linear chain with @node vs. declarative is a lateral move — the user saves the `nodes=[...]` list but pays the price of `sys.modules[__name__]` + `construct_from_module(...)`. Zero net DX win.

The actual selling points of @node are:
1. **Fan-in without multi-field wiring** — `def report(claims: Claims, scored: Scored, verified: Verified)` just works
2. **Refactor-safety** — rename a function, downstream parameter reference breaks at import time
3. **Topology-from-signatures** — out-of-declaration-order dependencies sort themselves

None of these are visible in a linear 3-node chain.

## Proposed

Either replace `01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py` with a fan-in showcase, OR add `examples/01c_decorator_fan_in.py` as a new file. The showcase should:

1. Define 3-4 producer @nodes that emit different types (e.g., `fetch_claims`, `score_claims`, `verify_claims`, `gather_metadata`).
2. Define one consumer @node with fan-in: `def report(fetch_claims, score_claims, verify_claims, gather_metadata) -> Report`.
3. Define the nodes in a SHUFFLED order (not topological) so `construct_from_module`'s topo-sort is visibly doing work.
4. Include comment lines showing:
   - Which @node produces what
   - How the fan-in parameter names resolve to upstream nodes
   - Why this is nicer than `Node.scripted(input={'fetch_claims': Claims, ...})` (the existing dict-input pattern)
5. Run end-to-end with `compile` + `run` and print the aggregated report.
6. Stay under 100 lines — it's an example, not production code.

Use schemas that motivate the shape: e.g. requirements analysis (Claims, Scores, Verification, Metadata → Report) or code review (Files, Lint, Security, Coverage → Review).

## Acceptance criteria

- New example file at `examples/01c_decorator_fan_in.py` (keep 01b as the simple intro; add 01c as the fan-in follow-up).
- `python examples/01c_decorator_fan_in.py` runs without errors and prints a meaningful report.
- Example has a docstring at the top explaining what it demonstrates and contrasting it with 01 (declarative) and 01b (linear decorator).
- Out-of-order declaration proves topo-sort works (put the `report` node FIRST in the file).
- No new tests required — this is documentation-as-code. The existing `test_node_decorator_fan_in_three_upstreams` already covers the code path under test.
- Keep the example under 100 lines including docstring and blank lines.

## Files to read

- `examples/01_scripted_pipeline.py` — the original reference
- `examples/01b_scripted_pipeline_decorator.py` — the linear port to beat
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestNodeDecorator::test_node_decorator_fan_in_three_upstreams` — the pattern to pull into example shape

## Non-goals

- Do NOT delete or modify 01 or 01b.
- Do NOT use LLM modes in the example — stays scripted to avoid fake-LLM setup noise.
- Do NOT block on other epic subtasks — this example showcases already-working functionality.

title: DI resolver returns None silently when FromInput param missing
description: When run(input={}) omits a param declared as Annotated[str, FromInput], the DI resolver returns None. The user function then crashes with an unrelated error (ValidationError, AttributeError). Should raise ExecutionError: 'FromInput param missing_param not found in input. Pass it via run(input={"missing_param": value})'. Guard: test that missing FromInput produces ExecutionError with helpful hint.
close_reason: Fixed: required=True default — missing FromInput now raises ExecutionError

title: Compile-time: verify tool factory registration
description: At compile(), for every node with mode=agent/act and tools=[Tool('x'),...], verify each tool.name exists in _tool_factory_registry. Currently crashes deep in the ReAct loop at runtime. One loop over construct.nodes in compile().
notes: ## Research context: No other Python LLM framework validates tool registrations at compile time. LangGraph, PydanticAI, CrewAI, DSPy all defer to runtime. BAML validates but requires a DSL. This is low-hanging fruit to claim the strongest compile-time guarantee in the space.

title: Dogfood: migrate no-dep test classes (TestScriptedPipeline, TestExecuteMode, TestMiniRWPipeline, basic produce/gather) to @node
description:
Part of epic neograph-eiw (Dogfood @node). Tests that don't use Oracle / Each / Operator / sub-construct.

## Scope

Migrate the following test classes in `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py` from declarative `Node(...)/Construct(nodes=[...])` to @node:

1. `TestScriptedPipeline` — scripted linear pipeline
2. `TestExecuteMode` — execute mode with mutation tools
3. `TestMiniRWPipeline` — mixed scripted + produce modes
4. Basic parts of any other test class that constructs scripted/produce/gather pipelines without modifiers

## What to do

1. For each test, replace `Node.scripted("x", fn="fn_name", ...)` + `register_scripted("fn_name", lambda ...)` with:
   ```python
   @node(mode='scripted', output=...)
   def x(...) -> ...:
       return ...
   ```
2. Build the pipeline via `construct_from_module(mod)` where `mod` is a fresh `types.ModuleType(...)` with the decorated functions attached as attributes (same pattern as `TestNodeDecorator._fresh_module`).
3. Preserve the test's assertions exactly.
4. Run the migrated tests incrementally: `python -m pytest tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestScriptedPipeline -x` etc.

## Acceptance criteria

- All four target test classes use @node exclusively for pipeline construction.
- Every test's assertion set is preserved or strengthened (never weakened).
- Full test suite stays green.
- If a migration reveals an @node gap, STOP that test's migration and file a new subtask on neograph-4mx.
- Tests for modifiers (Oracle, Each, Operator, raw) remain on declarative — separate subtasks handle those.

## Files to read

- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestScriptedPipeline, TestExecuteMode, TestMiniRWPipeline` — current patterns
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestNodeDecorator::_fresh_module` — throwaway module helper
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py::TestNodeDecorator::test_node_decorator_basic_chain` — end-to-end @node test pattern

## Non-goals

- Do NOT migrate `TestConstructValidation`, `TestConfigInjectionPatterns`, `TestErrorPaths` — they test the IR directly.
- Do NOT migrate Oracle / Each / Operator / raw / sub-construct test classes — separate subtasks.
- Do NOT delete `register_scripted` — it's still used by tests that keep declarative form.
close_reason: See prior close reasons. Force-closing because the subtask→epic dependency direction was set backwards at creation (subtask depends on epic instead of epic depends on subtask); cleanup will correct the direction across all epic subtasks in a single pass after this commit.

title: Establish baseline -- zero failures
description:

2. **Run integration tests** (if your project has them):

title: prompt_compiler silently falls through to generic fallback when template vars don't match — should warn or fail
description:
## Problem
When a node declares `prompt='uc/brainstorm-extensions'` and the template has placeholders like `{si_text}`, but the node's input parameters are named `write_si`, the prompt_compiler silently catches the KeyError from str.format() and falls through to the generic fallback prompt. The carefully crafted template with constraints, worked examples, and bad-example guidance is never used — and there's zero indication this happened.

This caused 2 production bugs in piarch where templates with critical quality constraints (step numbering, action granularity) were silently replaced by generic prompts.

## Expected behavior
When a template exists for a node's prompt name but compilation fails due to missing variables:
1. Log a WARNING with the template name, available vars, and missing vars
2. Optionally: fail hard in development/test mode (configurable)

The developer should know immediately that their template isn't being used.

## Where it happens
prompt_compiler callback registered via configure_llm(). The try/except at template compilation catches KeyError/TypeError and falls through to get_generator_prompt() without logging.

## Suggested fix
```python
try:
    prompt_text = get_prompt(template_name, **template_vars)
except (KeyError, TypeError) as exc:
    log.warning('prompt_template_fallback', template=template_name, error=str(exc), available_vars=sorted(template_vars.keys()))
    # fall through to generic
```

Even better: at construct compile time, validate that each node's input param names can satisfy its template's placeholders. This is a static check — parse {placeholders} from the template file and diff against the node's parameter names.
design:
## Fix Design

### Fix A: Inline prompt missing-var warning (_llm.py:209,217)
In _resolve_var, when parts[0] not in input_data, log structlog warning with var name + available keys. Still return '' (don't break compilation), but make it visible.

### Fix B: No action needed on neograph side
The prompt_compiler call at _llm.py:259 already passes through exceptions. The bug is in piarch's compiler catching KeyError — that's a piarch fix, not a neograph fix. Neograph's contract is correct: if the compiler raises, it propagates.

### Update test
Change test_inline_prompts.py:67 test_missing_key_returns_empty — verify warning is emitted (not just empty string).

Scope: ~5 lines production change + 1 test update.
notes:
## Reproduce Findings

### Two manifestations:

**A. Inline prompt (_resolve_var) — neograph-owned**
- _llm.py:209 — input_data.get(parts[0], '') silently returns empty string
- _llm.py:217 — getattr(obj, attr, '') same pattern
- Test at test_inline_prompts.py:67 explicitly validates silent behavior

**B. User prompt_compiler call — _llm.py:259**
- No try/except around _prompt_compiler() call
- Bug is that piarch's compiler catches KeyError internally and returns generic prompt
- Neograph can't detect this because compiler returns valid messages either way

### Fix approach
- Fix A: structlog warning in _resolve_var when var not in input_data
- Fix B: wrap _prompt_compiler call so exceptions propagate with context
- Recommendation: RAISE not just warn (0.x, one consumer, 2 prod failures)

### Core invariant
A prompt template must either resolve all placeholders to non-empty values, or emit a visible signal (warning/error) that resolution failed.

title: Test suite migration: split monolith into 9 files + adopt BDD naming
description:
Split test_e2e_piarch_ready.py (86 classes, 6800 lines) into 9 focused files. Adopt property-based naming (test_{what_happens}_when_{condition}) during migration. Seven phases, each a single commit of class moves. No test logic changes except renames.

Target layout:
- test_pipeline_modes.py (~40 tests)
- test_modifiers.py (~80 tests)
- test_validation.py (~80 tests)
- test_node_decorator.py (~70 tests)
- test_forward.py (~50 tests, merge existing file)
- test_composition.py (~30 tests)
- test_renderers.py (~60 tests)
- conftest.py (shared schemas + fixtures)

Delete migration artifacts: TestNodeInputsFieldRename (5), TestNodeOutputsRename (7), TestExtractInputListUnwrap (3) — behavior covered by integration tests.

Naming convention: test_{what_should_happen}_when_{condition}. Class docstrings as feature specs.

title: Establish baseline -- zero failures
description:
Run the full quality suite BEFORE any code changes. This establishes the
clean slate. Every failure after this point belongs to you.

## Instructions

1. **Run quality gate**:
   ```bash
   make quality 2>&1 | tail -5
   ```
   Record the exact pass count.

2. **Run integration tests** (if your project has them):
   Run the project's integration test command and record pass count.

## Gate -- HARD STOP on any failure

If ANY test fails in the baseline:
- Do NOT proceed to the next atom.
- Do NOT classify failures as "pre-existing" or "from another agent."
- Report the exact failures and leave this atom OPEN.

## Record Baseline

```bash
bd update neograph-9r1 --append-notes "## Baseline (pre-change)
- Quality check: [N] passed, 0 failed
- Integration: [N] passed, 0 failed
- Total: [sum] passed, 0 failed"
```
acceptance_criteria: All gates pass with zero failures. Exact counts recorded in epic notes.

title: Triage review findings for neograph-kqd.2
description:
Read the architect review and decide next steps for neograph-kqd.2.
This is a TRIAGE atom -- it routes, not executes.

### If ALL_LOW:
Note "Clean review -- proceeding to implement." Close this atom.

### If NEEDS_REFINEMENT:
Create a refine atom addressing MEDIUM findings.

### If NEEDS_USER_INPUT:
Block for human direction. Leave this atom OPEN.

## Key Principle
Triage ROUTES work. It does not DO work.
acceptance_criteria: Verdict acted on.

title: Write regression test for neograph-kqd.2
description:
Write a failing test that defines the expected behavior for neograph-kqd.2.
This is the TDD "red" step -- the test MUST exist and MUST fail before
any implementation begins.

## Instructions

1. Identify what to test from the implementation plan
2. Choose test level (prefer integration over unit when DB involved)
3. Write the failing test
4. Use test factories for test data (never inline model construction)
5. Run and confirm it fails for the RIGHT reason (AssertionError, not ImportError)

## BANNED Patterns

- `pytest.mark.xfail` is BANNED in regression tests
- AST-scanning / source-code pattern tests are BANNED
- Direct DB session management in test code is BANNED

## Verification: Paste the FAILED Output

You MUST see the word "FAILED" in pytest output.

## Store Test Reference

```bash
bd update neograph-kqd.2 --append-notes "## Regression Test
- Test: <test_path>::<test_name>
- Status: FAILING (expected -- TDD red step)
- Tests expected behavior: [one sentence]"
```
acceptance_criteria: Failing test exists. Test reference written to task notes.

title: Implement neograph-kqd.2
description:
Implement beads task neograph-kqd.2 -- make the failing test pass (TDD green),
then refactor.

## Workflow

1. **Green**: Write minimum code to make the failing test pass
2. **Verify**: Run the SAME test that FAILED -- must now PASS
3. **Refactor**: With passing tests as safety net
4. **Quality gate**: Run project quality command

## When EXISTING Tests Fail

1. STOP. Do NOT modify the failing tests.
2. Re-read the Core Invariant.
3. If your approach violates the invariant -> revert and rethink.
4. If the test has a genuine bug -> document WHY before modifying.

Sacred rule: NEVER adjust tests to match code without justification.

## If Successful

Close the original beads task:
```bash
bd close neograph-kqd.2
```
acceptance_criteria: Quality command passes. Regression test passes. Original task closed.

4. Verify: `git log -1 --oneline`

title: Research neograph-kqd.3
description:
Execute research for beads task neograph-kqd.3. All findings go into the
bead itself -- NOT the filesystem.

## Instructions

1. **Read the task**: `bd show neograph-kqd.3`
2. **Spec check** (if task touches schemas/protocol):
   - Read relevant specs and schemas
   - Check required vs optional, type constraints
3. **Explore the codebase**:
   - Search for relevant code
   - Read files that will need modification
   - Check existing tests for the affected area
   - Find 2-3 similar implementations to follow as patterns
4. **Check documentation** (doc-first rule)
5. **Engineering checklist** -- answer concretely:
   - DRY: Does similar logic exist?
   - Library idioms: How do your frameworks solve this?
   - Data flow trace: Walk one concrete example through the full chain
   - Existing conventions: What pattern do similar implementations follow?
   - Test infrastructure: What fixtures/helpers exist?
6. **Identify architecture decisions**
7. **Extract the core invariant** -- State in ONE sentence the architectural
   principle that ALL changes for this task must preserve.

## Store Research in the Bead

```bash
bd update neograph-kqd.3 --append-notes "## Findings
- [key findings]

## Relevant Code
- path/to/file.py:line -- [description]

## Risks & Edge Cases
- [potential issues]"

bd update neograph-kqd.3 --design "## Core Invariant
[One sentence: the architectural principle every change must preserve]

## Implementation Plan
1. [first step]
2. [second step]"
```

## Gate: Add Label

ONLY after notes AND design are written:
```bash
bd label add neograph-kqd.3 research:complete
```
acceptance_criteria: Task has research:complete label. Notes contain Findings. Design contains Core Invariant + Implementation Plan.

description: Oracle's merge_fn is a plain function with signature (variants, config) -> output. It can't use FromInput/FromConfig parameter injection like @node does. Example pain: def _group_claims_merge(variants, config) needs to manually extract shared = SharedResources.from_runnable(config) and node_id = config.get('configurable',{}).get('node_id'). Should be: def group_claims_merge(variants: list[X], shared: FromConfig[SharedResources], node_id: FromInput[str]) -> Y. Either add a @merge_fn decorator, or let merge_fn reference a @node-decorated function that's called in merge context rather than as a topology node.
close_reason: 6jd: FromInput[PydanticModel] and FromConfig[PydanticModel] now detected at decoration time and resolved at runtime by pulling each of the model's field names from config['configurable'], constructing the instance. Classifier walks call-stack frames to find locally-defined Pydantic model classes (needed under 'from __future__ import annotations'). Shared _classify_di_params + _resolve_di_value helpers extracted so both @node and @merge_fn use the same DI machinery. 4 regression tests in TestFromInputPydanticModel. 9zj: new @merge_fn decorator for Oracle merge functions with FromInput/FromConfig DI. Auto-registers via register_scripted for back-compat with Oracle(merge_fn='name') lookups. make_oracle_merge_fn in factory.py checks the @merge_fn registry first and calls with resolved DI args instead of legacy (variants, config) signature when a match is found. Plain (variants, config) merge_fn functions still work unchanged. 3 tests in TestOracleMergeFnDI covering bundled FromConfig, FromInput, and back-compat. 217/217 passing.

title: node_start log fields inconsistent across modes
description: factory.py: scripted logs input_type+output_type, produce logs only output_type, gather/execute log tools+budgets but no types. All modes should log input_type and output_type as base fields. Source: code review MED-03, CON-07.

title: Condition expression evaluator for Loop/Operator when clauses
description:
## What
Spec-driven pipelines express conditions as strings: when: 'score < 0.8'. Need a safe evaluator that runs these against Pydantic model field values.

## Approach
- Parse simple expressions: field_name op literal (e.g. 'score < 0.8', 'confidence >= 0.9', 'passed == false')
- Supported ops: <, >, <=, >=, ==, !=
- Field access: dotted path (e.g. 'result.score < 0.8')
- NO arbitrary code execution — whitelist of ops, no function calls, no imports
- Return a callable(value) -> bool that the Loop/Operator modifier can use

## Deliverable
- parse_condition(expr: str) -> Callable in a new module (src/neograph/conditions.py)
- Tests: valid expressions, type coercion (string '0.8' → float), bad expressions raise clear errors

title: Replace frame-stack walking with explicit namespace passing
description:
Replace frame-stack walking with explicit namespace passing.

METHODOLOGY: Write the structural guard test FIRST.

Step 1: Add a test to tests/test_structural_guards.py that greps for "frame_depth" in src/neograph/ and asserts 0 matches.

Step 2: In the @node decorator (decorators.py), capture caller namespace ONCE at the top: caller_ns = sys._getframe(1).f_locals

Step 3: Pass caller_ns explicitly to _classify_di_params and _build_annotation_namespace instead of frame_depth. Update their signatures.

Step 4: Remove _build_annotation_namespace frame-walking logic. Accept caller_ns directly.

Step 5: Run guard test — must pass.
Step 6: Run full test suite — must pass. Test with classes defined inside test methods (the case that required frame walking).

GATE: uv run pytest tests/test_structural_guards.py -v passes
GATE: grep -c "frame_depth" src/neograph/ returns 0
GATE: uv run pytest -q --tb=line passes
design: Change _build_annotation_namespace(f, frame_depth=2) to _build_annotation_namespace(f, caller_ns=None). Callers capture sys._getframe(1).f_locals once at decorator entry and pass explicitly. Remove frame_depth parameter. Keep closure var extraction. infer_oracle_gen_type passes None (already frame-independent). Core invariant: annotation resolution uses an explicit namespace, never walks the call stack.
notes:
MANDATORY: Step 1 of every task is writing a structural guard test in tests/test_structural_guards.py that FAILS before the work starts. The guard runs on every pytest invocation and is the acceptance criterion — not self-reports, not grep. The task is not done until the guard passes AND remains in the test file permanently.
## Research findings
- 7 call sites for frame_depth across 2 files (_di_classify.py, decorators.py)
- _build_annotation_namespace walks sys._getframe + 8 hops to find locally-defined classes
- Needed because from __future__ import annotations turns annotations to strings
- Fix: capture sys._getframe(1).f_locals ONCE at top of decorator(f), pass as caller_ns
- infer_oracle_gen_type uses frame_depth=0 (already frame-independent, uses closure vars)
- _source_location in _construct_validation.py is SEPARATE, not part of this task
- Risk: 8-hop walk catches types defined several scopes up; single capture may miss some
- Mitigation: in practice needed types are always in immediate caller scope
close_reason: frame_depth eliminated. Guard test_no_frame_depth_in_source passes. Explicit caller_ns captured once at decoration time.

title: Sub-construct output boundary check doesn't fire for single-node sub-constructs

The check exists in _construct_validation.py line 192-212 but the producers list appears empty or the check doesn't fire for this specific case. Fixture at tests/check_fixtures/known_gaps/subconstruct_output_mismatch.py reproduces the issue.

title: E2E test: YAML spec → compile → run → correct output
description:
## What
End-to-end integration test proving the full pipeline: write a YAML spec file, load it, compile it, run it, assert correct output.

## Test cases
1. Simple pipeline: seed → refine (scripted, loop_when)
2. Multi-node loop: sub-construct with Loop modifier
3. Oracle ensemble: models=['reason', 'fast'] with merge
4. Variable substitution: ${node.field} in prompts
5. Project surface: types auto-generated from JSON Schema defs

## Approach
Use scripted mode (no real LLM) so tests are fast and deterministic. Register scripted functions that the spec references by name.

## Deliverable
- tests/test_spec_loader.py with 5+ e2e tests
- All pass with uv run pytest
close_reason: 16 honest E2E tests. Found and fixed multi-node sub-construct wiring bug. 528 passed.

title: Trace similar patterns for neograph-mwx3
description:
Check if the same bug pattern exists elsewhere in the codebase.

1. Read the root cause from design field
2. Identify the bug pattern category
3. Search for the same pattern across the codebase
4. Document findings, file separate beads tasks if needed
acceptance_criteria: Similar pattern analysis written to task notes.
notes:
## Similar Pattern Analysis
- if/else: Handled via re-trace (record_branch). Works correctly.
- for-loop over proxy attr: Handled via __iter__/record_iteration → Each fan-out. Works for map-over-items, NOT for cycles.
- for-loop over range(): Python-level iteration, nodes deduped. No cycle.
- while: Goes through __bool__/record_branch (treated as branch, not loop). No cycle.
- try/except: Dead-code limitation (v1). Documented.
- Loop modifier: EXISTS in modifiers.py, compiler handles it. The missing piece is the tracer detecting for/while and lowering to Loop.
- No other files attempt loop-to-cycle compilation. The gap is entirely in the tracer.
close_reason: Analysis complete. The tracer handles if/else (re-trace) and for-over-proxy (Each fan-out) but NOT iterative refinement cycles. The Loop modifier exists in modifiers.py and the compiler handles it. The gap is the tracer not detecting for/while and lowering to Loop.

title: Architect review of neograph-mwx3
description:
Review the proposed fix for correctness, minimality, and risk.

## Review Criteria

1. Root cause correctness
2. Fix minimality
3. Regression risk
4. Architecture compliance
5. Similar bug scalability
6. Test coverage sufficiency

Rate findings: LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH.
acceptance_criteria: Review written with rated findings and overall verdict.
notes:
## Architect Review

### 1. Root Cause Correctness: LOW risk
The root cause is clear and well-scoped: _Tracer.record() deduplicates by id(node), so repeated calls in a for-loop are no-ops. No back-edge is generated.

### 2. Fix Minimality: HIGH risk
There is no minimal fix. The tracer architecture doesn't support cycle detection. Options range from documenting the limitation (zero code change) to a significant tracer rewrite (AST or new tracing pass).

### 3. Regression Risk: HIGH
Any tracer change risks breaking existing for-loop-over-proxy (Each fan-out) and if/else (branch re-trace) support, both of which are tested and working.

### 4. Architecture Compliance: MEDIUM
The project chose re-trace over AST. Options A and B push against this. Option C (explicit annotation) is architecturally compatible.

### 5. Similar Bug Scalability: LOW risk
This is the only pattern affected.

### 6. Test Coverage Sufficiency: LOW risk
The failing test is clear and will catch the regression.

## Verdict: NEEDS_REFINEMENT
The fix requires a design spike. The tracer needs architectural changes to detect loop structure, and the interaction with branch re-trace needs careful design. Recommend filing a separate design task and marking the existing test as documenting a known limitation.
close_reason: Architect review complete. Verdict: NEEDS_REFINEMENT. The fix requires tracer architecture changes (loop detection + branch interaction). No minimal fix exists. Recommend documenting as known limitation + design spike for loop cycle support.

title: Compiler: _add_loop_nodes with conditional back-edge
description:
Wire Loop modifier into the LangGraph graph with conditional back-edges.

## File: src/neograph/compiler.py

### In _add_node_to_graph (after Each check):

    loop = node.get_modifier(Loop)
    if loop:
        last_name = _add_loop_back_edge(graph, node, loop, prev_node, retry_policy=rp)
        if operator:
            last_name = _add_operator_check(graph, last_name, operator)
        return last_name

### New function _add_loop_back_edge:

def _add_loop_back_edge(graph, node, loop, prev_node, retry_policy=None):
    node_name = node.name
    node_fn = make_node_fn(node)
    field_name = node_name.replace('-', '_')
    count_field = f'neo_loop_count_{field_name}'

    graph.add_node(node_name, node_fn, retry_policy=retry_policy)

    if prev_node:
        graph.add_edge(prev_node, node_name)
    else:
        graph.add_edge(START, node_name)

    # Resolve condition
    if isinstance(loop.when, str):
        condition = lookup_condition(loop.when)
    else:
        condition = loop.when

    reenter_target = loop.reenter or node_name  # self-loop default

    def loop_router(state):
        count = getattr(state, count_field, 0)
        if count >= loop.max_iterations:
            if loop.on_exhaust == 'error':
                raise ExecutionError(f'Loop on {node_name} exceeded max_iterations={loop.max_iterations}')
            return END  # on_exhaust='last' — exit with last result
        # Call the condition
        should_continue = condition(state) if callable(condition) else condition(state)
        if should_continue:
            return reenter_target
        return END  # condition says stop

    graph.add_conditional_edges(node_name, loop_router)
    return node_name  # caller wires END edge

NOTE: The END routing must not conflict with the normal prev→next wiring. The loop_router returns either reenter_target (back-edge) or the NEXT node name (forward-edge). This needs careful integration with the compile() loop's prev_node tracking.

## Acceptance criteria
- Self-loop compiles and runs (test_self_loop_exits_when_condition_met)
- Multi-node loop compiles and runs (test_review_revise_cycle_exits_when_approved)
- max_iterations enforced (test_self_loop_respects_max_iterations)
- on_exhaust='error' raises ExecutionError (test_self_loop_raises_on_exhaust_error)
close_reason: Added Loop check in _add_node_to_graph and _add_loop_back_edge function with conditional back-edge routing, exit node, and max_iterations/on_exhaust handling

title: test_hypothesis.py: _build_scripted_surface is a 50-line mini-compiler with special cases
description: _build_scripted_surface has special-case branches for sub_construct, skip_when, and default topologies. This complexity in test infrastructure is a maintenance liability — bugs in the adapter mask framework bugs. Simplify: either make TopologySpec richer so the adapter is mechanical, or split into per-topology build functions.
notes:
## Audit Findings (agent 1/2)

### _build_scripted_surface (lines 2211-2274): 3 branches
1. **sub-construct** (24 lines): splits nodes into outer/inner, wraps in Construct. Does NOT call _apply_spec_modifiers — modifiers on sub-construct inner nodes silently dropped.
2. **skip-when** (23 lines): applies skip_when/skip_value via model_copy. Also does NOT call _apply_spec_modifiers — modifiers silently dropped.
3. **default** (11 lines): delegates to _apply_spec_modifiers. Handles bare/each/oracle/each_oracle/fan_in/deep_chain/loop.

### _apply_spec_modifiers (lines 2359-2376): 4 branches
each, oracle, each_oracle, loop. No else/default — unknown modifier string silently ignored.

### Surface sync status
| Topology | scripted | yaml | llm |
|----------|----------|------|-----|
| bare/each/oracle/each_oracle/fan_in/deep_chain | yes | yes | yes |
| loop | yes | no | yes |
| sub_construct | yes | no | no |
| skip_when | yes | no | no |

### Silent failure modes
1. New modifier string → _apply_spec_modifiers returns unchanged node (no error)
2. New meta-driven topology → falls through to default path (wrong behavior, no error)
3. YAML/LLM surfaces: sub_construct and skip_when unsupported, no error if routed there

### Simplification options
1. skip_when → NodeSpec field + _apply_spec_modifiers branch (eliminates 23-line special case)
2. _apply_spec_modifiers: add else raise ValueError (1 line, converts silent → loud)
3. sub_construct: extract to helper callable from all 3 surfaces
## Verification (round 2): DISCREPANCIES FOUND

1. Branch count confirmed correct (3 branches in _build_scripted_surface).

2. Sub-construct/skip-when skipping _apply_spec_modifiers: confirmed. No practical impact TODAY (those topologies never produce modifiers), but fragile — future combo would silently lose modifiers.

3. _apply_spec_modifiers no-else: confirmed. NodeSpec docstring comment stale — says 'each, oracle, each_oracle' but omits 'loop'.

4. NEW FINDING: _build_llm_surface calls _apply_spec_modifiers WITHOUT passing spec (line 2354). The loop branch falls back to 'default_cond' (unregistered) → runtime crash. Scripted surface correctly passes spec (line 2272). This is a second surface out of sync for loop handling, beyond YAML.

5. YAML surface confirmed: no loop code path. Loop topology via YAML adapter silently produces plain node without Loop modifier.
## Prep
- Post-56cn state: _build_llm_surface already deleted (finding #4 moot)
- Files to modify: tests/test_hypothesis.py
- Changes:
  1. NodeSpec.modifier docstring: add 'loop' to valid values (L1824)
  2. _apply_spec_modifiers: add else/raise for unknown modifier (L2281)
  3. _build_scripted_surface simplification: DEFER — branches are correct, just verbose; restructuring belongs in djhk/fg69 unification
- Core invariant: Test infrastructure must fail loudly on misconfiguration, never silently pass through
## Execution
- NodeSpec.modifier comment: added 'loop' to valid values
- _apply_spec_modifiers: added else/raise ValueError for unknown modifiers (converts silent pass-through to loud failure)
- Quality check: PASS (1309 passed, ruff clean)
close_reason: else/raise guard added, docstring fixed, 1309 tests pass

title: @node _validate_fan_in_types doesn't know about Each-wrapped dict output

When a downstream @node has parameter 'canonicalize: dict[str, RawClaim]' and upstream canonicalize has .map()/Each, _validate_fan_in_types says 'expects dict but upstream canonicalize produces RawClaim'. It should detect the Each modifier on upstream and unwrap the per-item type into dict[key_type, item_type] before type comparison. Location: neograph/decorators.py:_validate_fan_in_types around line 828.

The two walkers now agree. 4 regression tests in TestNodeDecoratorFanInEachInterop covering: dict[str, X] accepted, raw dict accepted, raw element type rejected, wrong dict element type rejected. 210/210 passing.

title: Each fan-out: dedup on duplicate keys with warning instead of crash
description:
Currently Each fan-out raises ExecutionError on duplicate keys. This is too strict — the LLM upstream may produce duplicates (e.g., same claim in two clusters). The framework should dedup by key (keep first), log a warning, and continue. The crash forces every consumer to dedup before fan-out, which is defensive boilerplate.

Current: raise ExecutionError('duplicate key R41')
Proposed: log.warning('each_duplicate_key', key='R41', kept_index=21, dropped_index=39) and skip the duplicate
design:
## Root Cause
Two locations raise ExecutionError on duplicate dispatch keys during Each fan-out:
1. compiler.py each_router guard (line 440-452) — pre-flight duplicate check before Send()
2. state.py _merge_dicts reducer (line 48-50) — reducer-time duplicate check

Both crash instead of deduping.

## Fix Approach
1. compiler.py each_router: On duplicate key, log warning with structlog (key, kept_index, dropped_index), skip the duplicate item (keep first). Continue dispatching only unique items.
2. state.py _merge_dicts: On duplicate key, log warning, keep existing value (first-write-wins = keep first).
3. Update 4 existing tests that assert ExecutionError to assert warning + dedup behavior instead.
notes:
## Reproduction
- Failing test: tests/test_modifiers.py::TestEachDuplicateKeyDedup::test_dedup_keeps_first_and_warns_when_each_has_duplicate_keys_node_api
- Failing test: tests/test_modifiers.py::TestEachDuplicateKeyDedup::test_dedup_keeps_first_and_warns_when_each_has_duplicate_keys_programmatic
- Root cause: Two locations raise ExecutionError on duplicate keys — compiler.py:451 (each_router guard) and state.py:50 (_merge_dicts reducer). Both should dedup (keep first) and log warning instead.

title: CRITICAL: Fix Construct(outputs=) → output= in 5 website pages
description: 5 pages use Construct(outputs=ScoredClaims) but the field is output= (singular). Pydantic extra=ignore silently drops it → compile fails. Pages: organizing.mdx, why-neograph.mdx, full-pipeline.mdx (x2), programmatic.mdx, overview.mdx.

title: oracle_gen_type inference fails when @merge_fn is defined AFTER @node in same file
description:
infer_oracle_gen_type runs at @node decoration time (line 652 in decorators.py). If the @merge_fn function is defined BELOW the @node in the same file, the merge_fn isn't in _merge_fn_registry yet. Inference returns None, oracle_gen_type stays None, and generators use the wrong type.

piarch's rw_ingestion.py:
```python
# Line 124: @node runs — group_claims not registered yet
@node(outputs=ClaimGroupingResult, ensemble_n=3, merge_fn='group_claims')
def decompose(read_node: ReadContext) -> ClaimGroupingResult: ...

# Line 128: @merge_fn runs — too late, decompose already has oracle_gen_type=None
@merge_fn
def group_claims(variants: list[DecompositionResult], ...) -> ClaimGroupingResult: ...
```

Result: oracle_gen_type is None. Generators ask LLM for ClaimGroupingResult (wrong). Should ask for DecompositionResult.

Fix options:
1. Defer inference to compile time (construct_from_functions/compile) instead of decoration time
2. Document that @merge_fn must be defined BEFORE the @node that references it
3. Two-pass: @node stores the merge_fn name, compile resolves it later

Option 1 is cleanest — compile() has access to all @node and @merge_fn registrations.
notes:
## Reproduction
- Test: test_oracle_gen_type_inferred_when_merge_fn_defined_after_node
- Status: FAILING — oracle_gen_type=None because @merge_fn not yet registered at @node decoration time
- Fix: move inference to _build_construct_from_decorated (construct assembly time)
## Trace: no similar issues — this is the only place inference happens at decoration time
## Review: [LOW] Move inference from @node decorator (L652) to _build_construct_from_decorated (after all nodes registered). Keep the decoration-time inference as a best-effort (when merge_fn IS already registered). Overall: ALL_LOW

title: Refactor _extract_input into shape dispatcher
description: 93-line branching monster in factory.py handles every input shape variant. Same treatment as classify_modifiers: match on input shape, dispatch to named helpers. InputShape enum + match + assert_never. Each helper is independently testable. Guard: _extract_input function body must be < 30 lines (delegates to helpers).
design:
## Core Invariant
_extract_input dispatches on input shape via named helpers — the main function is pure dispatch with no inline logic.

## Implementation Plan

### InputShape enum
```python
class InputShape(Enum):
    NONE = 'none'
    LOOP_REENTRY = 'loop_reentry'
    EACH_ITEM = 'each_item'
    FAN_IN_DICT = 'fan_in_dict'
    SINGLE_TYPE = 'single_type'
```

### classify_input_shape(state, node) → InputShape
Determines which branch to take. Priority order:
1. inputs is None → NONE
2. Loop modifier + own field populated → LOOP_REENTRY
3. neo_each_item present + matches type → EACH_ITEM
4. inputs is dict → FAN_IN_DICT
5. else → SINGLE_TYPE

### Named helpers
- _extract_none() → None
- _extract_loop_reentry(state, node) → extracted value
- _extract_each_item(state, node) → neo_each_item value
- _extract_fan_in_dict(state, node) → dict of upstream values
- _extract_single_type(state, node) → first matching field value

### Main function
```python
def _extract_input(state, node):
    shape = classify_input_shape(state, node)
    match shape:
        case InputShape.NONE: return None
        case InputShape.LOOP_REENTRY: return _extract_loop_reentry(state, node)
        case InputShape.EACH_ITEM: return _extract_each_item(state, node)
        case InputShape.FAN_IN_DICT: return _extract_fan_in_dict(state, node)
        case InputShape.SINGLE_TYPE: return _extract_single_type(state, node)
    assert_never(shape)
```

### Guard
_extract_input body must be < 30 lines (pure dispatch). AST-scan in test_structural_guards.py.
notes:
## Findings

### _extract_input: 93 lines, 5 dispatch branches
1. **None** (line 392): inputs is None → return None
2. **Loop re-entry** (lines 404-444): classify_modifiers → LOOP → read own append-list[-1]. Sub-branches for single-key dict, multi-key dict, non-dict.
3. **Each fan-out** (lines 447-449): neo_each_item in state + matches type → return item
4. **Fan-in dict** (lines 455-472): inputs is dict → read each key from state. Sub-branches for fan_out_param, loop unwrap, list-over-dict unwrap.
5. **Single type scan** (lines 474-483): iterate state fields, find matching type

### Call site
- Called once: factory.py:297 in _execute_node
- Internal function, not exported

### Existing helpers
- _state_get(state, key) — unified state access
- _is_instance_safe(val, type_spec) — safe isinstance
- _unwrap_loop_value(val, expected_type) — from di.py
- _unwrap_each_dict(val, expected_type) — from di.py
- classify_modifiers(node) → (ModifierCombo, mods) — from modifiers.py

### Pattern to follow
classify_modifiers in modifiers.py: enum + match + assert_never. Same approach for input shapes.

### Relevant code
- src/neograph/factory.py:390-483 (_extract_input)
- src/neograph/factory.py:297 (call site)
- src/neograph/modifiers.py classify_modifiers pattern

### Risks & Edge Cases
- Loop re-entry with multi-key dict is the most complex sub-branch (20 lines). Needs its own helper.
- The Each fan-out check (line 447-449) runs BEFORE the dict check — order matters because a dict-input node in an Each context should get neo_each_item, not the dict lookup.
- Single-type scan is a fallback — only fires when inputs is a bare type (not dict). Used by programmatic API nodes.
## Architect Review
- [LOW] classify_input_shape priority: Loop re-entry must check BEFORE Each item. The current code already has this order. Plan matches.
- [LOW] The EACH_ITEM shape check relies on neo_each_item being present AND matching the node's input type. This is correct — if the types don't match, fall through to FAN_IN_DICT.
- [LOW] assert_never is already used in modifiers.py for classify_modifiers. Consistent.

Overall: ALL_LOW
## Regression Tests
- tests/test_structural_guards.py::TestExtractInputDispatch::test_extract_input_body_is_short — FAILING (currently 93 lines, must be < 30)
- tests/test_structural_guards.py::TestExtractInputDispatch::test_input_shape_enum_exists — FAILING (InputShape enum doesn't exist yet)
close_reason: InputShape dispatch: 93 lines → 12 lines main + 5 helpers. Structural guard enforced.

title: Follow-up: evaluate Path B (underscore-prefix Node/Construct) after dogfooding
description:
Part of epic neograph-eiw (Dogfood @node). **Blocks on all other dogfood subtasks closing.**

## Purpose

The Path A positioning decision (2026-04-05) said: make @node the primary public API, keep `Node`/`Construct` importable as low-level IR. Path B (delete from public API entirely) was deferred pending migration data from the Dogfood epic.

Once all other dogfood subtasks close, this task runs the evaluation: given the migration data, should we take Path B?

## Inputs for the decision

After the dogfooding completes, answer these questions:

1. **Is there ANY example that still uses declarative `Node(...)` or `Construct(nodes=[...])` for a real pipeline pattern?** If no → Path B is safe; if yes → Path B would break that example.

2. **Is there any user-facing test class (not IR tests) that still uses declarative form?** If no → Path B is safe.

3. **What's the minimum viable set of IR classes the compiler + validator + state builder need internally?** List them.

4. **How many total lines of user-facing code import from `neograph` and reference `Node` or `Construct`?** Grep the migrated examples + tests for this.

5. **Would dynamic/programmatic construction (pipelines built from YAML, DB, plugin config) still work if `Node`/`Construct` were underscore-prefixed?** YES — advanced users can still `from neograph._node import _Node` or similar. The question is whether we want to SIGNAL that it's advanced by prefixing.

6. **The `TestConstructValidation` test class — does it still need to import `Construct` directly?** Yes, but it's in the test suite, not user code. Tests can reach into underscore-prefixed internals legitimately.

## Decision

Based on the answers, decide:

- **Take Path B**: rename `Node` → `_Node`, `Construct` → `_Construct` (file-level: `node.py` → `_node.py`, `construct.py` → `_construct.py`). Remove from `__init__.py` `__all__`. Update `_construct_validation.py`, `compiler.py`, `factory.py`, `state.py` imports. Update `TestConstructValidation` to import from the underscore paths. Update docs to remove all `Node`/`Construct` references for anything other than explaining the IR for framework hackers.

- **Stay Path A**: leave `Node`/`Construct` in the public API with the "advanced use" framing. Dogfooding showed the positioning works and the low-level API has legitimate user-facing use cases (document them).

## Deliverable

This task produces ONE of:

1. A new issue `neograph-???` (if Path B wins): "Execute Path B — underscore-prefix IR classes", with a full migration plan derived from the evaluation.
2. A short note on this issue's description (if Path A wins): explaining the decision and the cases that justified keeping public access, with pointers to them.

## Dependencies

- Blocks on every other dogfood subtask (neograph-4d9, 96y, uvb, 4ou, 5f?, r??, ?, ?) — run after all migrations complete.

## Files to read at evaluation time

- `src/neograph/__init__.py` — current exports
- `tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py` — post-migration test structure
- `examples/` — post-migration example set
- `src/neograph/_construct_validation.py`, `compiler.py`, `state.py`, `factory.py` — internal IR consumers

## Non-goals

- Do NOT pre-commit to Path B before the evaluation.
- Do NOT execute the Path B migration in this task — that's a follow-up if Path B wins.
close_reason: Path B evaluation: STAY ON PATH A. Dogfooding data confirms Node/Construct must remain public: (1) sub-constructs use Construct(input=X, output=Y) directly, (2) TestConstructValidation (22 tests) tests the IR validator via Construct(...), (3) ForwardConstruct subclasses Construct, (4) dynamic/programmatic construction from config is a legitimate use case. Node/Construct stay importable, documented as low-level IR for advanced use.

title: Loop modifier class in modifiers.py + __init__.py export
description:
Add Loop class to modifiers.py. Frozen Pydantic model inheriting Modifier.

## File: src/neograph/modifiers.py (after Operator class)

class Loop(Modifier):
    when: Any       # str (registered condition name) or Callable
    max_iterations: int = 10
    reenter: str | None = None  # back-edge target (None = self-loop)
    on_exhaust: Literal['error', 'last'] = 'error'
    history: bool = False

    def model_post_init(self, __context):
        if self.max_iterations < 1:
            raise ConfigurationError('Loop max_iterations must be >= 1')

## File: src/neograph/__init__.py
Add Loop to imports and __all__.

## Acceptance criteria
- Loop('cond') works (positional when)
- Loop(when='cond', max_iterations=5) works
- Loop(max_iterations=0) raises ConfigurationError
- All existing tests pass (Loop is not used anywhere yet)
